questions.stem: An electro-pneumatic control system with bellows (capacities C1, C2), flapper-nozzle (gain Kf), bellows area Ab and stiffness Kb, potentiometer sensitivity K1 V/m, amplifier gain K2 V/V, linear transducer gain K3 m/V, points P and Q connected (same displacement). Draw the block diagram of the system.
common_errors: Missing the pneumatic bellows dynamics (RC-type lag) in the signal path
Incorrect feedback path for the mechanical linkage connecting P and Q
Confusing pressure-to-displacement and displacement-to-pressure conversions
questions.solution_method: Identify each physical subsystem element (flapper-nozzle, bellows, amplifier, transducer, potentiometer) as a block with its gain or TF; trace the forward path from Xi to Pc and the feedback path through the transducer and potentiometer; apply the P=Q constraint to close the loop correctly.

questions.stem: For the electro-pneumatic system in Q1, determine the transfer function Pc(s)/Xi(s).
common_errors: Arithmetic errors combining the bellows and flapper-nozzle gains
Dropping the feedback gain K1*K2*K3 from denominator
questions.solution_method: Use the block diagram from Q1a; apply the standard closed-loop TF formula G/(1+GH) where G is the forward path gain product and H is the feedback path gain product; simplify algebraically.

questions.stem: For the electro-pneumatic system in Q1, determine the steady-state value of the output Pc for 3 units of sudden (step) input.
common_errors: Applying final value theorem to an unstable system without checking
Forgetting to multiply by input magnitude of 3
questions.solution_method: Apply the Final Value Theorem: lim_{s->0} s * [Pc(s)/Xi(s)] * (3/s) = 3 * lim_{s->0} Pc(s)/Xi(s); substitute s=0 into the TF derived in Q1b.

questions.stem: For the control system in Fig Q.2 with open-loop TF K*(s+α)/[s(s+5)] and feedback 1/(s+2), show the stable region in the K-α plane.
common_errors: Forming the wrong characteristic equation by not accounting for the inner feedback loop
Skipping the row of zeros special case in RH table
Plotting inequality boundaries incorrectly (open vs closed)
questions.solution_method: Find the closed-loop characteristic equation by simplifying the block diagram (forward: K(s+α)/[s(s+5)], feedback: 1/(s+2)); form CE = s^3 + 7s^2 + (10 + Kα)s + 2Kα + 10 = 0 (verify); apply RH criterion; derive inequalities on K and α for all-positive first-column elements; shade the stable region in the K-α plane.

questions.stem: For the system in Fig Q.2, find K and α for damping ratio ζ=0.707 and settling time ts=2 sec (2% criterion). Also sketch the real-axis locus of the roots.
common_errors: Using wrong settling-time formula (1% vs 2% band)
Confusing σ and ωd when locating the desired pole
Not verifying that the designed K is within the stable region
questions.solution_method: From ts=2 sec (2% band), σ = 4/ts = 2; from ζ=0.707, ωd = σ = 2, so desired poles at -2 ± j2; substitute s = -2+j2 into the characteristic equation to solve for K and α simultaneously; sketch real-axis root loci showing departure/arrival directions.

questions.stem: For the system in Fig Q.2, draw neat sketches of root contours for K=11.25 and K=18.25, clearly showing all steps.
common_errors: Treating root contours as standard RL (they are RL in α, with K fixed)
Incorrect pole/zero locations after substituting fixed K
Missing asymptote angles for the α-variation RL
questions.solution_method: Fix K and reformulate CE as 1 + α*[KN(s)/D(s)] = 0 to get root contour; identify equivalent open-loop poles and zeros; apply standard root locus rules (angles, asymptotes, breakaway) in s-plane for the variation in α.
key_formulas: Root contour: CE rearranged as 1 + α*G_eq(s) = 0

questions.stem: Sketch the Nyquist plot for a system with open-loop TF G(s) = K(s-3)/[s(s+2)(s+5)], choosing the appropriate Nyquist contour. Determine the range of K for which the closed-loop system is stable.
common_errors: Treating s-3 as a minimum-phase factor instead of a RHP zero
Incorrect encirclement count due to NMP zero affecting phase
Forgetting to account for the pole at origin with a semicircular indentation
Wrong open-loop RHP pole count P when applying N = Z - P
questions.solution_method: Note G(s) has a RHP zero at s=3 (non-minimum phase) and a pole at origin; indent Nyquist contour around origin; compute G(jω) for ω: 0+ to ∞; note phase goes from -90° to steep values due to NMP zero; find crossings of real axis; count encirclements of -1/K (or equivalently find K for N=0 so Z=0 closed-loop RHP poles); use N = Z - P with P=0.
key_formulas: Real-axis crossing: Im[G(jω)] = 0, solve for ω

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Fig Q.4 (autotransformer + electric actuator gain Ka V/V, thermal resistance R °C/W, thermal capacitance C J/°C, sensor Kt V/°C, Joule's coefficient J), draw the labeled signal flow graph.
common_errors: Incorrect Laplace-domain representation of the RC thermal lag (1/(RCs+1))
Omitting Joule's coefficient J in the power-to-heat conversion branch
Wrong feedback sign at the summing node
questions.solution_method: Write incremental equations: e_a = Ka*e_r; power W = J*e_a; ΔT(s) = W*R/(RCs+1); feedback: e_f = Kt*ΔT; error e_r = e_ref - e_f; draw nodes for each variable and edges with the gains/TFs above.

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q4, obtain the incremental transfer function ΔT(s)/Δe_r(s) using Mason's gain formula.
common_errors: Incorrect graph determinant Δ (missing feedback loop gains)
Not setting cofactor Δ1 = 1 when the forward path touches all loops
questions.solution_method: Apply Mason's gain formula: T = Σ(Pk*Δk)/Δ; identify the single forward path gain P1 = Ka*J*R/(RCs+1) and the single feedback loop gain L1 = -Ka*J*Kt*R/(RCs+1); compute Δ = 1 - L1; TF = P1/Δ.

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q4, derive expressions for the sensitivity of the system to changes in Ka in open-loop and closed-loop mode and reduce for DC conditions.
common_errors: Using wrong sensitivity definition (should be S = (dT/T)/(dKa/Ka))
Not setting s=0 for DC reduction
questions.solution_method: OL TF: T_OL = Ka*J*R/(RCs+1); S^T_Ka(OL) = 1. CL TF from Q4ii; compute S^T_Ka(CL) = 1/(1 + Ka*J*Kt*R/(RCs+1)); at DC (s=0): S_DC = 1/(1 + Ka*J*Kt*R), showing sensitivity reduction by closed-loop.

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q4, if inlet temperature Ti is also varied, draw the modified signal flow graph and determine ΔT(s)/ΔTi(s).
common_errors: Incorrect entry point for Ti disturbance into the SFG (should enter at the thermal node)
Not setting e_ref = 0 when finding ΔT/ΔTi (superposition)
questions.solution_method: Add a disturbance node Ti entering the tank's temperature node with gain 1 (or via thermal coupling); set reference input to zero; apply Mason's formula to find ΔT/ΔTi with the same feedback loop; the loop gain suppresses disturbance at DC.
key_formulas: Disturbance TF uses same denominator as CL TF

questions.stem: The open-loop TF of a unity negative feedback system is K/[s(1+2s)(0.1s+1)]. Design a phase-lead compensator using Bode (asymptotic) plots to meet: (a) steady-state error for unit ramp ≤ 20%, (b) phase margin = 40° (with 10° safety margin). Draw the complete Bode plots including compensator.
common_errors: Wrong Kv from ramp SSE requirement (SSE ≤ 20% ⟹ Kv ≥ 5)
Not adding 10° safety margin to required PM before choosing compensator phase
Computing α = (1-sinφ_m)/(1+sinφ_m) instead of reciprocal (pole-zero arrangement)
Incorrect gain crossover frequency shift after adding compensator gain
questions.solution_method: Step 1: SSE for ramp = 1/Kv ≤ 0.2 ⟹ Kv = K ≥ 5; set K=5. Step 2: Plot Bode of G(jω)=5/[jω(1+2jω)(1+0.1jω)]; find PM of uncompensated system. Step 3: Required additional phase = (40+10) - existing PM; compute φ_m; find α = (1-sinφ_m)/(1+sinφ_m); compensator gain = 1/√α; find new ωgc where |G(jωgc)| = -20log(1/√α) dB; set ωm = ωgc = 1/√α * (1/T); solve for T; compensator: C(s) = (1+αTs)/(1+Ts). Step 4: Draw composite Bode plot.
key_formulas: Kv = lim_{s→0} s*G(s) for type-1 system

notes: First Semester 2008-2009 paper (AAOC C321). Total marks 120 across 5 questions. Q1 involves an electro-pneumatic system with a hand-drawn schematic (image-only diagram); block diagram topology inferred from problem text. Q5 carries 30 marks and requires Bode graph sheets. NMP system in Q3 (RHP zero at s=3) is a notable difficulty.
